title: erigeron uintahensis
intro: Erigeron uintahensis, also known as Uinta fleabane, is a perennial herb belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae) and is native to the high-altitude mountainous regions of the western United States and southwestern Canada. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe wounds, reduce swelling, and prevent infection. Erigeron uintahensis has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve, and has also been used in traditional remedies to treat colds, sore throats, and skin irritations.
